notes: 0.13.0 SHIPPED 2026-07-21 (session 26-07-21-007, impl commit c39b836): prompt-chain integrity, CASP-PROMPT-007..010 -- the first rules to validate the QUEUE as an ordered plan rather than its head. Every prior PROMPT/SESSION rule covered one prompt (the head named by next_prompt) or the past (what shipped); the ordering of what had not run yet was a template convention with no rule behind it, so a queue could be silently unexecutable while check exited 0. THE SPLIT: dangling next_after (007) and cycle incl. self-reference (008) FAIL because they are claims that CANNOT be true -- the plan is unexecutable as written; fork (009) and unreachable-from-head (010) WARN because they are AMBIGUOUS rather than false, and a deliberate parking lot of queued-but-unchained prompts is a legitimate way to work. Carried over from the phase prompt's recommendation unchanged. OPT-IN, DERIVED FROM THE DATA, NO STATE KEY: the canonical template ships next_after as a literal placeholder, so a rule firing on it would have reddened every cockpit already on CASP. A placeholder, an empty string, null, and ANY non-string YAML value (number, float, bool, list, map, ~, whitespace) are not declarations and produce nothing; a repo where no queued prompt declares a real predecessor emits no finding at all, not even a PASS -- the CASP-SESSION-003 treatment, now the established shape for an opt-in rule. Skipped non-declaring prompts are counted in the PASS detail, never silent. RESOLUTION IS EXACT AFTER A DOCUMENTED NORMALIZATION: prompt stem / its lowercase form / its slug (scaffold PHASE- prefix removed), a session id via the CASP-SESSION-001 resolver, or a phase id from state. No edit distance anywhere -- CASP-SESSION-003 refused fuzzy filename matching and refusing it twice is the point of having a precedent. Identity collisions (A.md and PHASE-A.md both answer to 'a') resolve by explicit precedence over a path-sorted list, NOT by readdir order -- the first implementation let the filesystem decide, which would have let the same repo chain differently on two machines. Only queued prompts are subjects; a shipped prompt stays a valid TARGET but its own next_after is never re-litigated. No orphan storm: when there is no usable head, CASP-PROMPT-001/003 already FAIL and the reachability walk stays silent rather than burying the one actionable finding. status --json gains `queue` (resolved order, head first; null when unadopted or incoherent) -- additive, both schemas stay v1, status still exits 0 on a drifted chain. DOGFOODING FOUND THE FIRST BUG IN THIS REPO: on its first real fixture the rule reported two CASP-PROMPT-010 warnings and they were correct -- PHASE-FACTS-LAYER declared next_after: check-shipped-log (shipped as 0.11.0) and PHASE-DEMAND-GATED-TAIL declared PHASE-VERIFY-AND-STATE-DIFF (shipped long before). Neither was dangling; both resolved cleanly and simply encoded no order. The chain existed on paper. Both corrected; the queue now resolves prompt-chain-integrity -> facts-layer -> demand-gated-tail. OUT-OF-SCOPE FIX, TAKEN DELIBERATELY: the commissioned review of this slice never returned a report, so the three priority checks were re-run by hand by EXECUTION rather than reading, and found a pre-existing crash -- a non-string next_prompt (42, a list, an object) reached join() and threw ERR_INVALID_ARG_TYPE, killing the ENTIRE report with a raw stack trace before any other check ran. A gate that crashes is worse than one that reddens: CI reads it as infrastructure failure, not drift. Now a CASP-PROMPT-001 FAIL naming the type. Same class as the 0.12.1 scalar-state crash; next and status already coerced through String(), check was the one unguarded verb. Other adversarial probes passed: missing//file-shaped sessions dir, a directory named *.md among the prompts, missing session-logs/, phases_shipped holding non-strings, next_prompt escaping via ../../../etc/passwd, and three disjoint cycles each reported separately with the upstream node correctly excluded from the ring it feeds. 121 -> 146 tests. check --json schema stays 1, status --json schema stays 1, no existing finding changes verdict, a cockpit scaffolded before this release still PASSes (pinned by test). PHASE-FACTS-LAYER.md REFRESHED at close: it was drafted 2026-07-20 against 0.10.0 and four releases had shipped since. Its SHOULD asked a future session to make saveState atomic -- ALREADY DONE in 0.12.1 (temp + rename), so a fresh agent would have re-implemented shipped work; the section now says so explicitly and re-scopes the remaining half, which is the compare-and-swap the incident actually exhibited (atomicity protects a partial write, not an overwritten one). Stale line references (README.md:285 pointed at unrelated prose after two README rewrites) replaced with section names. Added a what-changed-since-drafting table: 0.11.0 and 0.13.0 are the two working precedents for derived adoption, and 0.12.0 is what UNBLOCKS the phase since casp/facts.json is a new cockpit-root file no existing repo could have received before upgrade existed. OPEN QUESTION FOR THE CEO: PHASE-FACTS-LAYER.md is written in French while casp-core is a public repo and a published npm package and every other prompt is English -- left as-is, not a drive-by language flip. NOT PUBLISHED TO NPM -- 0.11.0, 0.12.0 and now 0.13.0 are stacked; publishing stays a separate CEO-gated act. POST-PUSH REVIEW FIXES (commit 03a92b0, folded into 0.13.0 which is unpublished, not a 0.13.1): the commissioned adversarial review returned AFTER the first push with GO-WITH-FIXES and one real BLOCKER. Fork detection was keyed on the RAW next_after string -- the one site in chain.ts that never got the identity treatment -- so `next_after: PHASE-A` and `next_after: phase-a` looked like two predecessors instead of one slice. The CASP-PROMPT-009 WARN was missed, nothing else was wrong, so the chain was declared COHERENT and status --json's `queue` published a linear order asserting C runs after B when C declares it runs after A. A FALSE MACHINE-READABLE CLAIM is worse than a missed warning: a consumer of `queue` cannot tell it is wrong, and `queue` is the one field this release added for agents to consume. Fixed by keying forks on the RESOLVED target -- every identity of a slice shares one canonical key (path for a prompt, the string itself for a terminal); messages print each claimant's own spelling so the report never shows a canonical name the operator never typed. Three smaller fixes same review: a cycle also emitted an orphan WARN per ring member (one defect reported N times, contradicting the missing-head guard's own principle); the skipped count lived only in the PASS line so it vanished exactly when the report was non-trivial, against the spec's explicit 'never silent' (a scope line is now emitted either way); and a stale comment describing the pre-tiering resolver. 146 -> 151 tests. PROCESS NOTE, SAME MISTAKE AS 0.12.0: the push went out before the review returned. Green build, passing tests, semantic defect. Recoverable only because nothing was published -- had npm publish been bundled into this session a false `queue` would have shipped. Do not push a new rule category ahead of its commissioned review.
